tasks.name: Open the most cited CC BY neuroscience article published between 2021–2022 and view its PDF
tasks.description: Search for neuroscience articles, filter by publication years 2021–2022 and CC BY license, sort by citation count, and open the PDF of the most cited article.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main search bar in the header.
Type "neuroscience" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
In the filters sidebar, set the publication year range by entering "2021" as the minimum year and "2022" as the maximum year.
In the "Access type" or "License" filter section, select "Open Access" and then choose the "CC BY" license option.
Open the sort dropdown above the results list and select "Citations - High to Low".
Click on the title of the first article in the sorted results list (the one with the highest citation count).
On the article page, click the "Download PDF" or "View PDF" button to open the article’s PDF.

tasks.name: Create a reading list of 3 highly cited climate change articles in Environmental Science from the last 5 years
tasks.description: Find climate change impact articles in Environmental Science from 2019–2024, select those with more than 50 citations, and save 3 of them into a dedicated reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the main search bar.
Type "climate change impact" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
In the publication year filters, set the minimum year to "2019" and the maximum year to "2024".
In the subject area filter section, select the "Environmental Science" checkbox.
Open the sort dropdown above the results and choose "Citations - High to Low".

On the first search result that shows more than 50 citations, click its "Save to Reading List" or bookmark icon and create a new list named "Climate Impact Review" (or select it if it already exists).
Scroll down the results and find the next article with more than 50 citations; click its "Save to Reading List" icon and add it to the "Climate Impact Review" list.
Continue scrolling and select a third article that shows more than 50 citations, then click its "Save to Reading List" icon and add it to the same "Climate Impact Review" list.
Open the user or profile menu in the header, click on "Reading Lists" (or similar), and then click on "Climate Impact Review" to open the list containing the 3 saved articles.

tasks.name: Choose a computer science journal for submission with APC ≤ $1500 and review time ≤ 60 days and open its submission guidelines
tasks.description: Browse computer science journals, compare APC and median review time for the first three, select the first journal that meets the APC and review time thresholds, and open its submission guidelines page.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Journals" link in the main navigation bar.
In the journals listing page, use the subject filter to select "Computer Science".
Use the journals sort dropdown to select "Impact Factor - High to Low".
Click on the title of the first journal in the filtered list to open its journal information page and note its Article Processing Charge (APC) and median review time values.
Use the browser’s back button or the provided navigation link to return to the computer science journals list.
Click on the title of the second journal in the list and review its APC and median review time values in the metrics section.
Return again to the journals list and click on the title of the third journal, then review its APC and median review time values.

From the three journals you just inspected, identify the first one in the list whose APC is $1500 or less and whose median review time is 60 days or less, and click its title to open its journal page once more.
On the selected journal’s page, click the "Submission Guidelines" or "For Authors" button/link in the header area to open the submission guidelines page.

tasks.name: Export APA citations for the two largest-sample cardiology randomized controlled trials
tasks.description: Search for cardiology randomized controlled trials, filter to clinical trials in cardiology, sort by sample size, and copy APA citations for the two largest studies.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the search bar and type "randomized controlled trial cardiovascular" then press Enter.
In the article type filter section, select the "Clinical Trial" option.
In the subject area filter, select "Cardiology".
Open the sort dropdown above the results and choose "Sample Size - High to Low" (or the equivalent largest-study-first option).
Click on the title of the first article in the sorted results list to open its article page.
On the article page, click the "Cite" or citation icon, select the "APA" citation style, and then click the "Copy citation" or similar button to copy the APA citation.
Use the "Back to results" link or browser back button to return to the sorted results list.

Click on the title of the second article in the list and, on its article page, click the "Cite" icon again, choose "APA" if needed, and click the "Copy citation" button to copy the second APA citation.

tasks.name: From a 2023 journal issue, open a long review article and save its HTML full text to a reading list
tasks.description: Browse to a 2023 issue of a specific journal, locate a review article spanning at least 11 pages, open its HTML full text, and add it to a dedicated reading list.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Browse" menu and select "By Journal".
In the journals list, use the search field to type "Journal of Environmental Studies" and click on that journal’s name to open its journal page.
On the journal page, click the "Issues" tab.
In the year dropdown or navigation, select the year "2023" and then click on "Volume 12, Issue 4" (or the first 2023 issue available).
On the issue contents page, use the article type filter to select "Review Articles".
From the list of review articles, identify an article whose page range shows a span of at least 11 pages (for example, pages 100–112) and click its title to open the article page.
On the article page, click the "Full Text HTML" or "View HTML" link to open the HTML version of the article.

In the article’s toolbar, click the bookmark or "Save to Reading List" icon and add the article to a reading list named "Env Studies Reviews 2023" (creating the list with that name if it is not already present).

tasks.name: Create a weekly search alert for recent machine learning in healthcare articles
tasks.description: Run a search on machine learning in healthcare, restrict results to 2020 and later in relevant subjects, sort by newest, and set up a weekly on-site alert named "ML Health Weekly".
steps: On the homepage, click in the main search bar, type "machine learning healthcare patient outcomes", and press Enter to run the search.
In the publication year filter, set the minimum year to "2020" so only recent articles are included.
In the subject filters, select both "Medicine" and "Computer Science" to focus on interdisciplinary work.
Use the sort dropdown above the results to select "Publication Date - Newest First".
Near the top of the results page, click the "Create Alert" or "Save Search" button.
In the alert form, type "ML Health Weekly" into the "Alert name" field.
In the frequency dropdown, select "Weekly" and then click the "Save Alert" or "Create" button to finalize the alert.

tasks.name: Find and open the details page of an open CSV dataset linked from a gene expression article
tasks.description: Search for gene expression dataset articles with open data, open one such article, navigate to its data section, and open the details page of a CSV-format dataset.
steps: From the homepage, click the search bar and type "gene expression dataset" then press Enter.
In the filters, select the "Research Article" option under article type.
In the "Open Science" or data availability filter section, check the option labeled "Open Data Available".
Use the sort dropdown to select "Relevance" so the most relevant articles appear first.
In the results list, click on the title of the first article whose summary card shows an "Open Data" badge.
On the article page, click on the "Data & Materials" or "Supplementary Data" tab.
In the list of data resources, locate a dataset with a format label indicating "CSV" and click on that dataset’s title link.
On the dataset’s landing page, click the "View file list" or similar button to see the files associated with the dataset.

tasks.name: Build a bibliography collection of 5 urban air pollution articles from 2018–2023 from at least 3 different journals
tasks.description: Search for urban air pollution health effect articles from 2018–2023, filter by relevant subjects, and add 5 articles from at least 3 distinct journals into a single collection.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click on the search bar.
Type "urban air pollution health effects" into the search field and press Enter.
In the publication year filters, set the minimum year to "2018" and the maximum year to "2023".
In the subject area filters, select both "Public Health" and "Environmental Science".
Use the sort dropdown to select "Relevance" so that the most pertinent articles appear first.
Click on the title of the first article in the results list to open its article page.
On the article page, click the "Add to collection" or "Add to bibliography" button and create a new collection named "Urban Air 2018-23", adding this article to that collection.

Use the "Back to results" link or navigation to return to the search results, then sequentially open additional articles from the results so that you add four more articles from at least two different additional journals; for each selected article, click "Add to collection" and add it to the "Urban Air 2018-23" collection until you have added a total of 5 distinct articles.
After adding the fifth article, open the user or profile menu in the header and click on "Collections" or "My Bibliographies".
In the collections list, click on "Urban Air 2018-23" to open the collection and display the articles assembled for the literature review.

entities.name: Article
entities.description: Scholarly article with metadata, access info, metrics, and links to full text and datasets.

fields.name: authors
fields.description: Array of author display strings in order (e.g., 'Smith J.', 'Doe A.')

fields.name: journalId
fields.description: ID of the publishing journal

fields.name: issueId
fields.description: ID of the journal issue containing this article

fields.name: pageCount
fields.description: Total pages; can be derived from pageEnd - pageStart + 1 where available

fields.name: subjectAreas
fields.description: Array of subject area enum values: neuroscience, environmental_science, public_health, medicine, computer_science, cardiology

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: Array of free-form keyword strings

fields.name: isOpenAccess
fields.description: True if the article is open access

fields.name: hasOpenData
fields.description: True if associated datasets are available (drives 'Open Data' badge)

fields.name: openDataBadgeLabel
fields.description: Display label for open data badge, e.g., 'Open Data'

fields.name: sampleSize
fields.description: Total sample size for clinical / randomized controlled trials

fields.name: dataTabAvailable
fields.description: True if the 'Data & Materials' or 'Supplementary Data' tab should be shown

fields.name: datasetIds
fields.description: Array of dataset IDs associated with this article

entities.name: Journal
entities.description: Academic journal hosting multiple issues and articles, with metrics like impact factor and APC.

fields.name: subjectAreas
fields.description: Array of subject area enum values: neuroscience, environmental_science, public_health, medicine, computer_science, cardiology

fields.name: apcAmount
fields.description: Article Processing Charge amount in apcCurrency units

fields.name: apcCurrency
fields.description: Currency code, e.g., 'USD'

fields.name: medianReviewTimeDays
fields.description: Median time to first decision in days

fields.name: isOpenAccess
fields.description: True if the journal is fully open access

fields.name: submissionEmail
fields.description: Generic contact / submission email

fields.name: websiteUrl
fields.description: External journal homepage if different from platform

entities.name: JournalIssue
entities.description: Specific volume/issue of a journal containing a set of articles.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Human-readable label like 'Volume 12, Issue 4'

fields.name: articleIds
fields.description: IDs of articles contained in this issue

entities.name: Dataset
entities.description: Open dataset associated with an article, including file details and license.

fields.name: hasCsvFiles
fields.description: True if at least one associated file is in CSV format

fields.name: fileCount
fields.description: Number of files in the dataset

fields.name: isOpenData
fields.description: True if dataset is openly accessible

fields.name: fileIds
fields.description: Array of DatasetFile IDs associated with this dataset

entities.name: DatasetFile
entities.description: Individual file within a dataset, such as a CSV file.

fields.name: isPrimary
fields.description: True if this file is highlighted as the main file

entities.name: ReadingList
entities.description: Named list of articles to read later (e.g., 'Climate Impact Review'). Single implicit user.

entities.name: ReadingListItem
entities.description: Membership of an article in a specific reading list.

entities.name: Collection
entities.description: Bibliography or collection for literature reviews (e.g., 'Urban Air 2018-23'). Single implicit user.

fields.name: exportNotes
fields.description: Optional notes about how/why this collection will be used or exported

entities.name: CollectionItem
entities.description: Membership of an article in a specific collection/bibliography.

entities.name: SavedSearchAlert
entities.description: Saved search with alert configuration (e.g., 'ML Health Weekly'). Single implicit user.

fields.name: queryString
fields.description: Raw search query text (e.g., 'machine learning healthcare patient outcomes')

fields.name: publicationYearMin
fields.description: Lower bound for publication year filter

fields.name: publicationYearMax
fields.description: Upper bound for publication year filter

fields.name: subjectAreas
fields.description: Array of subject area enum values used in the alert filter

fields.name: articleTypes
fields.description: Array of articleType enum values used in the alert filter

fields.name: accessTypes
fields.description: Array of accessType enum values used in the alert filter

fields.name: licenses
fields.description: Array of license enum values used in the alert filter

fields.name: hasOpenData
fields.description: If true, filter to articles with open data; null/undefined if not applied

fields.name: isActive
fields.description: Whether the alert is currently active

entities.name: SubmissionGuidelines
entities.description: Submission guidelines and author instructions for a specific journal.

fields.name: contentHtml
fields.description: Full HTML/text content of the submission guidelines page

fields.name: apcSummary
fields.description: Natural language description of APCs for this journal

fields.name: reviewTimeSummary
fields.description: Natural language description of expected review timelines

fields.name: acceptedArticleTypes
fields.description: Array of articleType enum values accepted by the journal

fields.name: acceptedLicenses
fields.description: Array of license enum values accepted by the journal

fields.name: externalSubmissionUrl
fields.description: Link to external submission system, if applicable
